perturbation.original_text: Definitions: Unless otherwise defined in this Agreement, the fol lowing terms shall have the following meanings: Affiliate of the Party and/or any person specified in this A greement, means: (i) its parent company, its founder (participant or shareholder), including a physical person; and/or (ii) any company directly or indirectly controlled by the Party and/or its parent company. Confidential Information means any information and documents (whether or no t designated as confidential), including, without limitation, finan cial, tax, commercial, corporate, legal, technical, operational, management, business and other informa tion and documents concerning the Target or any of their Affiliates, as well as any and all informatio n and documents regarding the intended sale of the Target, that is directly or indirectly disclosed, f urnished or made available, whether before or after the date hereof, in any way or by any means whatsoever (including, without limitation, in writing, orally or in electronic form), in a data room or elsewhere, to t he Receiving Party or any employees of the Receivin g Party, by any director, manager, agent, representat ive, advisor or employee of the Disclosing Party or any Affiliate of the Disclosing Party, in connection wi th the intended sale of the Target. Confidential Information may include, without limitation, trade secrets, know-how, inventions, technical data or specifications, testing methods, Target or financia l information, research and development activities, product and marketing plans, and customer and suppl ier information. For the sake of clarity, the fact that the Seller is considering selling the Target, and t he existence and contents of this Agreement, are de emed to be Confidential Information. Target include: [ Name of the company ], a company duly incorporated and validly existing under the laws of [ ●] whose registered office is at: [ ●], registered under No. [ ●].
perturbation.changed_text: Definitions: Unless otherwise defined in this Agreement, the fol lowing terms shall have the following meanings: Affiliate of the Party and/or any person specified in this A greement, means: (i) its parent company, its founder (participant or shareholder), including a physical person; and/or (ii) any company directly or indirectly controlled by the Party and/or its parent company. Confidential Information means any information and documents (whether or not designated as confidential). Target include: [ Name of the company ], a company duly incorporated and validly existing under the laws of [ ●] whose registered office is at: [ ●], registered under No. [ ●].
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Confidential Information' has been removed. Section 2 states 'The Receiving Party undertakes to use Confidential Information only in direct connection with the Transaction.' Without a clear definition of what constitutes Confidential Information, it is unclear what information the Receiving Party is restricted from using, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Receiving Party undertakes to use Confidential Information only in direct connection with the Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information'. The changed text omits this definition. The contradicted text (Section 2) states 'The Receiving Party undertakes to use Confidential Information only in direct connection with the Transaction'. Without a definition of 'Confidential Information', Section 2 becomes unenforceable because there is no clear understanding of what the Receiving Party is restricted from using. This omission directly contradicts the enforceability of Section 2, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the perturbation type 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 3. The Receiving Party undertakes not to disclose Conf idential Information to other persons, except the employees of the Receiving Party listed in Anne x 1 to this Agreement that need to possess such information in order to achieve the purposes s pecified in Section 2 of this Agreement and who have signed confidentiality agreements or are o therwise obliged to maintain confidentiality in relation to the Confidential Information to the extent specified in this Agreement. The Receiving Party undertakes to keep Confidential Inf ormation separate from other information that belongs to the Receiving Party and/or third parties . The Receiving Party undertakes to take all possible measures that are necessary to prevent and avoid disclosure and/or loss of any Confidential Information. In any case, such measure s should comply with the measures that the Receiving Party would take for proper handling and protection of its own confidential information, and thus it must be the safety level t hat is not less than may reasonably be necessary. In the case of unauthorised disclosure and/or loss of Confidential Information, the Receiving Party must immediately inform the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Receiving Party undertakes not to disclose any information to other persons, except the employees of the Receiving Party listed in Anne x 1 to this Agreement that need to possess such information in order to achieve the purposes s pecified in Section 2 of this Agreement and who have signed confidentiality agreements or are o therwise obliged to maintain confidentiality in relation to the information to the extent specified in this Agreement. The Receiving Party undertakes to keep information separate from other information that belongs to the Receiving Party and/or third parties . The Receiving Party undertakes to take all possible measures that are necessary to prevent and avoid disclosure and/or loss of any information. In any case, such measure s should comply with the measures that the Receiving Party would take for proper handling and protection of its own information, and thus it must be the safety level t hat is not less than may reasonably be necessary. In the case of unauthorised disclosure and/or loss of information, the Receiving Party must immediately inform the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'information' this section is now contradicting the initial intention of the document, which is about the Non-disclosure of confidential information. Now, it can be interpreted as not disclosing any type of information, creating an in-text contradiction with the scope of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS: I. The Disclosing Party is an [advisor to a potential buyer] who is willing to, either itself or through its Affiliate(s) (as defined below), acquire, directly or indirectly, shares in the Target (as defined bel ow) or assets owned by the Target (the “ Transaction ”). II. In view of the proposed Transaction, the Disclosin g Party has agreed to disclose to the Receiving Par ty certain Confidential Information (as defined below) , and the Receiving Party has agreed to maintain secrecy in relation to such Confidential Informatio n.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Confidential Information" with "information" in section 3. This broadens the scope of non-disclosure beyond confidential information, contradicting the "WHEREAS" clause which specifically states that the Receiving Party has agreed to maintain secrecy in relation to Confidential Information. This creates a direct conflict because the agreement is explicitly about confidential information, not all information. Therefore, it's a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to the preservation and nondisclosure of the Confidential Information under this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect without being limited in time, except for cases when the Disclosi ng Party provides its written consent as to any reduction of said term.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to the preservation and nondisclosure of the information under this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect without being limited in time, except for cases when the Disclosi ng Party provides its written consent as to any reduction of said term.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'information,' this section broadens the scope of nondisclosure beyond what was originally intended. Given that 'Confidential Information' is no longer clearly defined, this creates an ambiguity about what specifically needs to be protected and contraditing Section 9 where exceptions on what cannot be confidential information are stated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Provisions of this Agreement do not refer to the Co nfidential Information which: • at the time of disclosure (or transfer/announcement ) to the Receiving Party, was or became generally known or published without the Rec eiving Party's breach of this Agreement, provided that the Receiving Party may sp ecify the source of such publicly known information; Sample of the Non-disclosure Agreement Budapest-943242.1 3 • at the time of disclosure (or transfer/announcement ) under this Agreement, was already known to the Receiving Party and was not received b y the Receiving Party under the terms of confidentiality, provided that the Receivi ng Party can prove the same; and • was received by the Receiving Party lawfully from a third party after its disclosure (transfer, announcement) by the Disclosing Party pu rsuant to the terms of this Agreement, provided that the Receiving Party can prove the sam e.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the scope of 'Confidential Information' to simply 'information.' This contradicts Section 9 because Section 9 provides exceptions to what constitutes 'Confidential Information,' which is now undermined by the change, as the agreement no longer clearly defines what information is protected and thus what exceptions apply, creating an inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, t he Receiving Party undertakes to promptly return to the Disclosing Party, or, at the Disclosi ng Party’s discretion, destroy all the Confidential Information (together with all copies and extracts) stored by the Receiving Party and/or its representatives (including without limitation, on c omputer disks and/or other digital media). Within five (5) calendar days from the date of term ination (expiration) of this Agreement, unless there was a written request from the Disclosing Par ty, the Receiving Party shall destroy all Confidential Information (together with all copies and extracts) stored by the Receiving Party and/or its representatives (including without limit ation, on computer disks and/or other digital media). In case of destruction of Confidential Info rmation under the terms of this Section, the Receiving Party shall send to the Disclosing Party a written confirmation of such destruction, which should contain a list of the destroyed Confid ential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, t he Receiving Party undertakes to promptly return to the Disclosing Party, or, at the Disclosi ng Party’s discretion, destroy all the documents and data (together with all copies and extracts) stored by the Receiving Party and/or its representatives (including without limitation, on c omputer disks and/or other digital media). Within five (5) calendar days from the date of term ination (expiration) of this Agreement, unless there was a written request from the Disclosing Par ty, the Receiving Party shall destroy all documents and data (together with all copies and extracts) stored by the Receiving Party and/or its representatives (including without limit ation, on computer disks and/or other digital media). In case of destruction of documents and data under the terms of this Section, the Receiving Party shall send to the Disclosing Party a written confirmation of such destruction, which should contain a list of the destroyed documents and data.
perturbation.explanation: This edit changes 'Confidential Information' to 'documents and data'. By doing so, the agreement becomes internally contradictory. While the agreement is framed as protecting only 'Confidential Information' according to WHEREAS, this section now mandates the destruction of *all* documents and data. It contradict Section 9 as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS: I. The Disclosing Party is an [advisor to a potential buyer] who is willing to, either itself or through its Affiliate(s) (as defined below), acquire, directly or indirectly, shares in the Target (as defined bel ow) or assets owned by the Target (the “ Transaction ”). II. In view of the proposed Transaction, the Disclosin g Party has agreed to disclose to the Receiving Par ty certain Confidential Information (as defined below) , and the Receiving Party has agreed to maintain secrecy in relation to such Confidential Informatio n.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information' in Section 11, aligning with the WHEREAS clause, which defines the agreement as protecting only 'Confidential Information.' The changed text broadens this scope to 'documents and data.' This creates a contradiction because Section 11 now mandates the destruction of *all* documents and data, even those not considered confidential, which directly conflicts with the WHEREAS clause's explicit focus on Confidential Information only. This is a strong contradiction as it alters the core scope of the agreement, making it unclear which information is truly protected and subject to the destruction clause. It aligns with the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition, as essential language ('Confidential Information') is altered, creating a contradiction with other sections.

perturbation.original_text: 13. In case the Receiving Party breaches this Agreement , the Disclosing Party, the Target or any of their Affiliates are entitled to receive from the R eceiving Party compensation of financial and moral damages, suffered by the Disclosing Party, th e Target or any of their Affiliates respectively, as a result of such breach by the Rec eiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. In case the Receiving Party breaches this Agreement , the Disclosing Party, the Target or any of their Affiliates are entitled to receive from the R eceiving Party compensation of financial and moral damages, suffered by the Disclosing Party, th e Target or any of their Affiliates respectively, as a result of such breach by the Receiving Party, provided that such breach relates to the unauthorized disclosure of the Target's name to the general public.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the proviso regarding disclosure of the target's name to the general public, this severely restricts the scope of breaches that can lead to compensation. Other breaches, such as misuse of confidential financial information, would no longer be covered. This contradicts the earlier sections of the agreement, which suggest a broader scope of protected information. This contradicts Section 3 where is mention that the Reciving party should keep confidential information separate from other information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The Receiving Party undertakes not to disclose any information to other persons, except the employees of the Receiving Party listed in Anne x 1 to this Agreement that need to possess such information in order to achieve the purposes s pecified in Section 2 of this Agreement and who have signed confidentiality agreements or are o therwise obliged to maintain confidentiality in relation to the information to the extent specified in this Agreement. The Receiving Party undertakes to keep information separate from other information that belongs to the Receiving Party and/or third parties . The Receiving Party undertakes to take all possible measures that are necessary to prevent and avoid disclosure and/or loss of any information. In any case, such measure s should comply with the measures that the Receiving Party would take for proper handling and protection of its own information, and thus it must be the safety level t hat is not less than may reasonably be necessary. In the case of unauthorised disclosure and/or loss of information, the Receiving Party must immediately inform the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 13 allows compensation for any breach of the agreement. The changed text in Section 13 limits compensation only to breaches related to unauthorized disclosure of the Target's name. This contradicts Section 3, which requires the Receiving Party to keep information separate and take measures to prevent disclosure of *any* information, not just the Target's name. The changed text significantly narrows the scope of breaches eligible for compensation, creating a direct conflict with the broader confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 3.

perturbation.original_text: 16. All notices and documents sent by the Parties to ea ch other under this Agreement shall be made in writing, sealed (if applicable) by the respectiv e Party and delivered either by (i) courier and/or a representative of the Party concerned (in this ca se they are deemed received from the moment the representative of the Party to which such notic e/document was sent, confirms receipt of the Sample of the Non-disclosure Agreement Budapest-943242.1 4 notification/document with his signature, indicatin g the date and time of receipt), or (ii) insured letter by mail to the post address of the Party con cerned, as indicated at the end of this Agreement (in this case, such letters are considered to be re ceived after three (3) calendar days from the date of posting).
perturbation.changed_text: 16. All notices and documents sent by the Parties to ea ch other under this Agreement shall be made via email to each party's listed email address.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the notification method, moving from physical documents to email. This introduces an internal contradiction as the agreement specifies signed and sealed documents, which are inconsistent with electronic communication. This contradicts Section 17 as well
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. This Agreement is made in two identical copies in t he English language with both having equal legal force and comes into effect once signed by th e authorised representatives of the Parties and sealed by the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that notices and documents are to be delivered in writing, sealed, and either by courier/representative with signed confirmation or by insured letter. The changed text mandates email communication for all notices and documents. This directly contradicts Section 17, which requires the agreement to be signed and sealed. Email communication cannot fulfill the 'signed and sealed' requirement, creating a direct conflict and unenforceability regarding the agreement's execution and notification process.
